CounterOneLabel.Name: First window text
CounterOneLabel.Hint: For example: Players Advantage, Party Advantage, or Players.

CounterTwoLabel.Name: Second window text
CounterTwoLabel.Hint: For example: Enemies Advantage, Opposition Advantage, or Enemies.

AllowPlayersCounterOne.Name: Players can change the first window
AllowPlayersCounterOne.Hint: The GM can always change both values. This option allows or blocks regular players from changing the first counter.

AllowPlayersCounterTwo.Name: Players can change the second window
AllowPlayersCounterTwo.Hint: It is often useful to keep the second window under GM control if it tracks enemy Advantage.

AllowNegativeValues.Name: Allow negative values
AllowNegativeValues.Hint: Disabled by default because Advantage usually cannot go below 0. Enable this if you use the module for another resource.

ResetOnCombatStart.Name: Reset when Combat is created
ResetOnCombatStart.Hint: If enabled, the active GM automatically resets both counters to 0 when a new Combat is created.

AutoOpenWindows.Name: Open windows on login
AutoOpenWindows.Hint: Client setting: affects only the current user.

ShowLauncher.Name: Show the Adv button
ShowLauncher.Hint: Client setting: the small button in the lower left opens or hides the windows.

UI.LockedText: Only the GM or permitted players can change this value.
UI.ToggleWindowsTitle: Open or hide Imp Counter windows
UI.OpenCounterOneTitle: Open or hide the first counter
UI.OpenCounterTwoTitle: Open or hide the second counter

Notifications.NoGM: Imp Counter: an active GM is required to save this world value change.
Notifications.NoPermissionWindow: Imp Counter: you do not have permission to change this window.
Notifications.NoPermissionCounters: Imp Counter: you do not have permission to change the counters.
